page_content: CHBE476 Molecular Modeling Methods (3 Credits) Statistical mechanics will be introduced to give the fundamental background for atomic to mesoscale molecular modeling. Classical atomic-level simulations methods (Monte Carlo and Molecular Dynamics) and the procedures to develop intra- and intermolecular potentials will be covered. This course will also discuss the theory and application of coarse-grained molecular simulations, mesoscale simulations and other modern simulation techniques. A broad range of applications will be included throughout the semester, e.g., phase behavior of small molecules, kinetics, and biophysics. Jointly offered with: CHBE476. Restriction: Permission of ENGR-Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering department. Credit Only Granted for: ENCH648P, CHBE476 or CHBE676. Formerly: ENCH468P.
